meta.name: Lomo Saltado (Peruvian Stir-Fried Beef)
meta.subtitle: Chifa-style beef + onions + tomatoes with soy-vinegar pan sauce and fries

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: SSA canonical home method: hot pan, quick stir-fry, sauce reduction, fries served alongside or mixed at the end.
Designed to be adaptable: steak type, fries method (oven/air fryer/frozen), and gluten-free (tamari) option.

summary: Classic Peruvian chifa favorite: seared beef strips tossed with onions and tomatoes, finished with soy + vinegar pan sauce and cilantro. Served with fries and rice.

yield.notes: Serves 4 with rice + fries; 3 if served alone.

signals.batchFriendly: Sauce components can be prepped ahead; final stir-fry is best fresh.
signals.freezerFriendly: Freeze cooked beef/veg only if needed; texture is best fresh. Fries should be made fresh.

heat.note: Ají amarillo is optional; keep mild or boost heat to taste.

optional.label: Deep fryer
optional.notes: Optional for traditional fries; oven or air fryer works well.

notes: Contains soy if using soy sauce.
For gluten-free: use tamari or certified GF soy sauce.

ingredients.label: Beef sirloin (or flank/strip steak), sliced into strips
ingredients.notes: Slice against the grain into 1/2-inch strips. Pat dry before searing.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust based on soy sauce saltiness; season beef lightly, then balance sauce.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Optional: subtle warmth; not always used, but common in many home versions.

ingredients.label: Red onion, thick wedges
ingredients.notes: Cut into 1/2-inch wedges; keep chunky so it stays crisp-tender.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes, thick wedges
ingredients.notes: Use ripe but firm tomatoes; cut into wedges. Add late to avoid turning mushy.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar (or white vinegar)
ingredients.notes: Peruvian versions often use vinegar for the signature tang.

ingredients.label: Beef or chicken stock (or water)
ingredients.notes: Optional: helps build a glossy pan sauce and deglaze.

ingredients.label: Sugar (or honey)
ingredients.notes: Optional: balances vinegar sharpness; omit if you prefer tang-forward.

ingredients.label: Ají amarillo paste
ingredients.notes: Optional: adds Peruvian chile fruitiness. Increase to 1 tbsp for more heat.

ingredients.label: French fries (fresh or frozen)
ingredients.notes: Cook separately: oven, air fryer, or deep fry. Traditional: fries tossed in briefly at the end; for crispness, serve alongside.

ingredients.label: Cooked white rice, for serving
ingredients.notes: Very common to serve fries + rice together in classic lomo saltado.

steps.title: Cook the fries first (keep them crisp)
instructions: Cook fries using your preferred method: oven, air fryer, or deep fry.
Aim for extra crisp so they hold up if you decide to toss them with the stir-fry.
Hold fries warm while you cook the beef.
checks: Fries are deeply golden and crisp.
Fries are held warm (not steaming in a sealed container).

steps.title: Prep the sauce and mise en place
instructions: In a small bowl, mix soy sauce, vinegar, optional stock (or water), optional sugar, and optional ají amarillo paste.
Cut onion and tomatoes into thick wedges; mince garlic; chop cilantro.
Pat beef strips dry and season lightly with salt and pepper (and optional cumin).
checks: Everything is prepped before heating the pan (stir-fry moves fast).
Beef is dry on the surface for best sear.

steps.title: Sear the beef in batches (very hot pan)
instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over high heat until very hot.
Add 1 tbsp oil. Sear beef in a single layer (work in 2 batches if needed).
Cook 45–75 seconds per side until browned but still medium-rare inside; remove to a plate.
checks: Beef is browned on edges, not steamed.
Beef is removed promptly to avoid overcooking.

steps.title: Stir-fry onions and aromatics, then add tomatoes
instructions: Reduce heat slightly to medium-high if pan is smoking excessively; keep it hot.
Add remaining 1 tbsp oil if needed.
Add onion wedges; stir-fry 1–2 minutes until edges char slightly but onions stay crisp-tender.
Add garlic and stir 15–20 seconds until fragrant.
Add tomato wedges and toss 30–45 seconds—just to warm and lightly soften, not break down.
checks: Onions still have bite (not fully soft).
Tomatoes are warmed but hold their shape.

steps.title: Deglaze with sauce, return beef, finish with cilantro
instructions: Pour in the sauce mixture; scrape up browned bits (fond) to build flavor.
Return beef (and any juices) to the pan; toss 30–60 seconds to glaze everything.
Turn off heat; toss in most of the cilantro.
checks: Sauce lightly coats beef and vegetables (not watery).
Beef remains tender (not cooked to well-done unless you prefer).

steps.title: Serve with fries and rice (two common styles)
instructions: Classic style: pile rice on one side of the plate, lomo saltado on the other, and fries on top or alongside.
Tossed-fries style (more traditional but less crisp): add fries to the pan for 10–15 seconds and toss to coat lightly, then serve immediately.
Finish with remaining cilantro and a squeeze of lime if desired.
checks: If tossing fries, serve immediately to avoid sogginess.
Taste and adjust: add a splash more vinegar for tang or soy for salt.

refrigerate.notes: Store beef/veg separate from fries if possible. Reheat beef/veg quickly in a hot pan.

reheat.notes: Reheat stir-fry in a hot skillet 2–3 minutes. Re-crisp fries in air fryer/oven.

leftoverUses: Beef stir-fry over rice bowl with extra cilantro
Wraps/tacos with onion + tomato + sauce drizzle

notes: Key success factor: HOT pan + small batches. Overcrowding steams the beef and vegetables.
If you want more sauce: add 1–2 tbsp stock/water and reduce briefly; keep the flavor balance soy-forward with vinegar tang.
For a deeper chifa vibe: add a tiny splash of oyster sauce (not traditional in all versions) or a pinch of MSG—optional.
Use 1.5 lb boneless chicken thighs, sliced.
Sear until cooked through (165°F / 74°C), then proceed as written.
Use tamari or certified gluten-free soy sauce. Check fries seasoning for gluten-containing additives.
Increase ají amarillo paste to 1 tbsp.
Add thin-sliced fresh chile (jalapeño or serrano) with onions.

beefServingTempNote.notes: Beef can be served medium-rare by preference; choose safe sourcing and handling. If you prefer fully cooked, sear longer and toss longer after sauce.

notes: Use separate cutting board/knife for raw beef and vegetables or wash thoroughly between.
Cook fries safely per package directions if frozen.
